content.text: Answer FOUR questions in detail. State carefully any results used without proof.

problem.text: Let \((f_n)\) be a sequence of continuous functions from \([0,1]\) to \([0,1]\). For each of the following, give a proof (if true) or a counterexample (if false):

subparts.label: (i)
subparts.text: if \(f_n(t)\) decreases pointwise to \(0\) then \(\int_0^1 f_n(t)\,dt \to 0\);

subparts.label: (ii)
subparts.text: if \(f_n(t)\) only converges pointwise to \(0\) then \(\int_0^1 f_n(t)\,dt \to 0\).

problem.text: For \(0<t<1\) and for \(n\) a positive integer, let \(f_n(t)=t^n\).

subparts.label: (i)
subparts.text: Show that the sequence \((f_n)\) converges uniformly on each compact subset of \((0,1)\).

subparts.label: (ii)
subparts.text: Show that \((f_n)\) does not converge uniformly on \((0,1)\).

problem.text: Let the function \(f:[1,\infty)\to\mathbb{R}\) be continuous, with \(\lim_{t\to\infty}f(t)=A\in\mathbb{R}\). Prove there exists a sequence \((p_n)\) of polynomials such that \(p_n(1/t)\to f(t)\) uniformly for \(t\geq 1\).

Let \(A\subseteq[0,1]\) have positive Lebesgue measure: that is, \(\lambda(A)>0\). Prove that if \(0<\alpha<\lambda(A)\) then there exists a Lebesgue measurable set \(A_\alpha\subseteq A\) such that \(\lambda(A_\alpha)=\alpha\).
State the Monotone Convergence Theorem and ‘Fatou’s Lemma’. Deduce one of these from the other.
